source_jobs.title: Industrial/Commercial Electrician Estimator
source_jobs.description:
Type: Full-Time, Remote (LATAM)

Working Hours: Mondays - Fridays, 8:00am - 5:00pm EST

Location: LATAM (Remote)

Compensation: USD $2,000 - $2,500/month (depending on seniority)


About the Company

Our client is an established electrical contracting company based in New Jersey. Known for quality craftsmanship and reliability, they specialize in residential and commercial electrical solutions, including advanced power systems and modern energy upgrades. They operate with a lean, execution-focused team that values high accountability and seamless remote collaboration.



Job Description

We are seeking a detail-oriented and experienced Remote Electrician Estimator / Pre-Construction Support Specialist to join our team. In this critical role, you will own the pre-construction administrative lifecycle and perform accurate material takeoffs that directly impact project profitability. The ideal candidate brings a strong technical background and extensive experience estimating electrical projects for the U.S. market. Working directly within the existing workflows, you will streamline vendor outreach, manage project documentation, and help scale the company’s bidding capacity.



Responsibilities

 - Perform detailed material and quantity takeoffs from plans and specifications, specifically focusing on panel & service upgrades, EV charger installations, generator installations, and general electrical maintenance.

 - Manage pre-construction administrative duties, including comprehensive bid preparation, compiling submittal packages, managing RFIs, and tracking electrical code revisions.

 - Review project documentation thoroughly to identify missing information, flag potential discrepancies, and clarify scope before formal submission.

 - Lead vendor outreach and subcontractor coordination to source accurate pricing, compile material quotes, and clarify project scopes.

 - Track active bid invitations, maintain the estimating pipeline, and ensure all submissions meet strict project deadlines.

 - Maintain a high-volume bidding cadence, efficiently managing data and documentation within the team's existing systems.



Project Types

 - Panel & service upgrades

 - EV charger installations

 - Standby and portable generator installations

 - Commercial & industrial electrical maintenance

 - Modern electrical pre-construction packages



Requirements

 - 5+ years of experience in electrical estimating, with a proven track record of handling commercial and industrial electrical projects for U.S.-based contractors.

 - Educational Background: Bachelor’s degree or professional background in Civil Engineering or a closely related technical field.

 - Core Expertise: Demonstrated mastery of electrical takeoffs, material counting, and labor-hour assessment.

 - End-to-End Bid Support: Solid understanding of the pre-construction pipeline—from initial plan review and vendor quote compilation to submittals and post-award administrative workflows.

 - Communication & Alignment: Exceptional, fully fluent English communication skills (written and verbal) for seamless internal collaboration and professional vendor outreach.

 - Schedule: Ability to work full-time hours strictly matching the US Eastern Time Zone (EST).

source_jobs.title: Digital Ad Buyer
source_jobs.description:
Job Title: Digital Ad Buyer & Quality Control

Location: Remote (Latin America)

Work Hours: Full-Time

Compensation: $2,500–$3,000 USD/month



About the Role

We are seeking a highly detailed and hands-on Digital Ad Buyer & Quality Control specialist to support our performance marketing team. Our client is a boutique digital marketing agency that prioritizes substance over fluff, maintaining a close, high-impact relationship with a limited roster of premium clients across music, entertainment, media, and tech.

This role is structured in phases: initially, your primary focus will be performing meticulous quality checks and reporting across paid marketing, email, and SMS campaigns. Within a month, you will transition into independently planning, setting up, and launching ad campaigns across multiple platforms. If you are an incredibly organized stickler for detail who loves a fast-paced environment, this role is for you.



Key Responsibilities

 - Quality Assurance: Quality check campaign execution by the advertising team, catching errors in setup, targeting, copy, creative, event dates, locations, and more.

 - Communications & Tracking: Monitor and respond to pertinent email communications between clients and external teams.

 - Campaign Execution: Build, launch, and manage end-to-end paid ad campaigns without assistance across Meta and TikTok (and ideally Google).

 - Strategy & Reporting: Create strategic and budget recommendations for campaigns and develop regular reports with actionable insights for clients.

 - Project Management: Ensure meeting action items are carefully noted, assigned, and tracked in ClickUp to ensure timely execution.



Must-Have Qualifications

 - Experience: 3+ years of experience building and managing ad campaigns, with deep expertise in the nuances of the Meta ads platform at a micro level, alongside experience in TikTok and Google Ads.

 - Project Management Tools: Proficiency using in-depth project management platforms such as ClickUp, Asana, or similar software.

 - Extreme Attention to Detail: Absolute zero-tolerance for typos, inaccurate information, or mismatched event details (such as dates, venues, or lineups).

 - Communication Skills: Proactive internal and external communication skills with fluent English proficiency.

 - Team Fit: Experience working closely with a small, fast-paced team while remaining highly organized with your own tasks, priorities, and calendar.



Nice-to-Have Qualifications

 - Experience working with ticketed live events, a passion for music, or strong taste in the space.

 - Hands-on technical experience with tracking pixels (generation, installation, conversion events, and custom audience creation).

 - Proficiency with Google Analytics to monitor client traffic trends.

 - Experience drafting and executing email or SMS marketing campaigns using platforms like Mailchimp, Klaviyo, Laylo, Hive, or Community.

 - Foundational SEO optimization skills across client web domains.



Why Work With Us?

 - Join a boutique, fast-paced team where your work directly moves the needle for high-quality clients.

 - Escape the corporate fluff—we focus on meaningful campaigns, proactive insights, and real results that keep clients partnering with us for years.

 - Opportunity to rapidly expand your operational responsibilities from quality control into full campaign ownership.

source_jobs.title: Residential Construction Estimator
source_jobs.description:
Type: Full-Time, Remote (LATAM) - Month-to-Month contract

Working Hours: Mondays - Fridays, 8:00am - 5:00pm EST (or standard U.S. business hours)

Location: LATAM (Remote)

Compensation: USD $1,500 - $2,500/month (depending on seniority)



About the Company

Our client is a dynamic U.S. construction and development firm specializing in large-scale residential communities and infrastructure projects. Known for building high-quality neighborhoods, townhouses, and expansive community amenities, they manage high-volume development pipelines. They operate with a collaborative, remote-forward team structure that values precision, high internal accountability, and seamless integration into their established pre-construction workflows.



Job Description

We are seeking a skilled and highly analytical Remote Construction Estimator to join our team as a dedicated, long-term contractor. In this role, you will not function as a detached third party; instead, you will become an integrated member of our organization, mastering our internal processes and owning the pre-construction estimation funnel. Your primary focus will be delivering highly professional, accurate quantity breakdowns and takeoffs for massive community developments. This is an excellent opportunity for a detail-oriented professional to manage complex, multi-million-dollar project scopes within a stable U.S. construction framework.



Responsibilities

 - Perform meticulous, detailed quantity takeoffs from architectural, structural, and civil construction plans for large-scale residential and community projects.

 - Prepare comprehensive, professional material and quantity breakdowns in Excel format, delivering clean data structures ready for final pricing assignments.

 - Lead vendor outreach to proactively source, track, and update material pricing, product data, and material specifications.

 - Manage essential pre-construction documentation, including processing RFIs (Requests for Information) and compiling submittal packages.

 - Track project timelines and deliverables across the entire estimation funnel, ensuring deadlines are met for fast-moving bidding cycles.

 - Calculate specialized civil and site requirements, including precise linear footage and area calculations for extensive community infrastructure.



Project Types

 - Master-planned community developments (250+ residential units with scopes ranging from $35M–$55M)

 - Single-family homes, townhouses, and multi-family residential units

 - Clubhouses, fitness hubs, and community amenity centers

 - Recreational facilities, including pickleball courts, entry gates, and electronic gate systems

 - Large-scale landscaping packages (sidewalks, sod, pavers, and complex irrigation systems)



Requirements

 - Experience & Track Record: Proven professional estimation experience specifically focused on residential, civil infrastructure, or community development projects for the U.S. market.

 - Educational Background: A degree in Civil Engineering, Architecture, or a closely related technical field is highly preferred.

 - Core Expertise: Exceptional mastery of material calculations, quantity takeoffs, and translating complex blueprints into highly organized Excel spreadsheets.

 - Software Proficiency: Advanced skills in Microsoft Excel and hands-on experience with standard digital takeoff software.

 - Communication & Alignment: Fluent English communication skills (written and verbal) for seamless daily collaboration, internal reporting, and vendor relations.

Job Title: Senior Auditor

About the Company:
We are a growing CPA firm in Texas managing over 3,000 clients annually across various segments (individuals, SMBs, and other business types). We pride ourselves on delivering quality audit and tax services while building a collaborative, supportive team culture.

 

About the Role:

We are seeking an experienced CPA with a strong audit background to join our accounting firm as a Senior Auditor. You will lead audit engagements from start to finish, supervise junior audit staff, and manage multiple client engagements across diverse industries. This is a hands-on role that requires ownership, independence, and a commitment to quality work.

In addition to strong audit expertise, this role requires a forward-thinking mindset. Our firm is actively implementing new technologies across audit, tax, and firm-wide operations. Candidates must be comfortable working in a tech-enabled environment and demonstrate either experience with modern tools or a strong willingness to learn and adopt them quickly.

Key Responsibilities:

 - Lead and manage audit engagements independently, taking ownership from initiation through completion

 - Conduct audits for commercial and non-profit clients across various industries

 - Manage 15-20+ concurrent audit engagements, with workload fluctuating seasonally

 - Execute audit work using AuditFile and AuditSight software

 - Collaborate with internal tax team on integrated engagements

 - Proficiency with audit software (AuditFile or similar platforms preferred but not required)

 - Fluent in English

Preferred Qualifications:

 - US GAAP and IFRS experience is a plus

Key Competencies:

 - Strong communication and interpersonal skills

 - Ability to take constructive feedback and incorporate it

 - Comfortable working as part of a team with regular interaction across departments

 - Problem-solving and critical thinking

 - Adaptability to new tools, systems, and AI-driven workflows

 - Proactive interest in improving processes through technology adoption

source_jobs.title: Project  Manager (Marketing)
source_jobs.description:
Project Manager (Marketing)

 - Type: Full-Time, Remote

 - Working Hours: Mondays - Fridays, 9:00am - 5:00pm CST

 - Location: LATAM (Remote)

 - Compensation: USD $2,000 - $2,500/month (based on experience)


ABOUT THE COMPANY:

For over 35 years, we've transacted more than $3.5 billion of real estate, acquiring over 14,648 multifamily units and taking dozens of properties full cycle — on average returning 100% of investor capital plus strong returns. Our focus is acquiring older apartment home communities and creating value by improving the properties, optimizing operations, and fostering human flourishing in each one. Above all, we've learned that success is measured not just by financial gain, but by the quality of the relationships we build along the way. Please read our values https://wearewilkinson.com/about/real-estate-investment-group-values, and if you are aligned, we encourage you to apply.

 


JOB OVERVIEW:

We're a US-based investment firm running two brands — an SEC-regulated real-estate investment fund and a family-focused legacy coaching and publishing brand. The Traffic and Compliance Manager is the keeper of time and project tracking. We can’t afford to delay deliverables, we need help with scheduling tasks and keeping track of where the projects are in the lifecycle. And with a remote team, everyone has to remain accountable for the commitments they make of their time and turnaround of the project or process.

Success is measured two ways: every day and priority keeps moving with no wasted days, the distributed marketing team is held accountable for its hours, and every published asset is correct, on-brand, and compliant.

 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own the status system and traffic flow. Own the Dailies/status tracker so every project stays visible and moving; route briefs, assets, and approvals between the EVP, the pod, and vendors; attend Manager meetings (or review the AI notes) to log tasks and commitments; set daily and weekly priorities and protect deadlines before they slip; maintain and refine the approval, routing, and publish workflows. Be the watchdog, the eagle eye, the person “in the know”.

 - Be the second set of eyes — nothing ships with an error. Receive a test ofevery email and SMS end-to-end (links, tags, opt-outs, routing, real user experience); verify UTMs on every link, email, and ad against the taxonomy before launch; proof spelling, grammar, check links and documents they link to, and functionality on everything; QA every form (it submits, tags correctly, assigns territory, notifies the right rep, lets leads book); review and approve staging builds before go-live. Ask: Is this the right user experience for this audience? Is it done with excellence?

 - Steward the regulatory approval chain. Ensure fund communication artifacts are reviewed by legal and the right parties are copied; monitor and flag new disclosure language; secure principal sign-offs on brand claims; enforce the initial creative gate so nothing advances without the EVP's sign-off (strict the first four months); steward each piece through the full chain — writer → principals → reviewer → board — and chase every handoff; route approved creative to SMEs, reviewer, and board, logging each approval; keep the approval record for every published asset; maintain one canonical, version-proofed source of truth so nothing ever pulls an outdated or non-compliant statement from the offering documents. You have authority to manage this system and process with whatever tools would work best, however, get input from others who have to use the system.

 - Own the last mile — publish and version control. Push approved assets live in the publishing stack (Notable, Planable, HubSpot, or equivalent); enforce version control so the current version is live and older ones are archived; oversee who has access to branded assets.

 - Hold the cadence and the team accountable. Keep the distributed (LatAm) marketing team accountable for hours

source_jobs.description:
Job Title: Construction Estimator – Commercial

Employment Type: Full-Time, Remote

Working Hours: Monday – Friday, 7:30 AM – 4:00 PM EST

Compensation: USD $1,500 – $2,200/month (salary based on assessment)

Location: LATAM



Company Overview

We are a U.S.-based commercial general contracting company specializing in retail, restaurant, office, medical, hospitality, and tenant improvement projects nationwide. Our team works closely across preconstruction, estimating, and project management to deliver high-quality construction solutions from bid to build.



We are built on a collaborative, fast-paced environment where accuracy, coordination, and accountability are key. Our focus is on delivering consistent results for repeat clients and national brands through strong subcontractor partnerships, detailed estimating, and efficient project execution.



Position Overview

We are seeking a detail-oriented and experienced Construction Estimator to join our general contracting team in a full-time remote capacity. In this role, you will be embedded within our organization, working closely with project management and pre-construction teams to support national bidding and estimation efforts across the United States.



You will play a key role in preparing accurate cost estimates, coordinating subcontractor pricing, and supporting end-to-end bid preparation for commercial construction projects.



Key Responsibilities

 - Perform detailed quantity takeoffs and material analysis from construction drawings and specifications

 - Prepare accurate and competitive cost estimates for commercial bid submissions

 - Review, analyze, and prequalify subcontractor bids to ensure full trade coverage and pricing competitiveness

 - Coordinate with subcontractors and sub-trade partners to obtain complete and up-to-date pricing

 - Support the full bid preparation and submission process

 - Collaborate with project managers during contract review and post-award project setup

 - Manage RFIs (Requests for Information) and submittals during the preconstruction phase

 - Use PlanHub and other estimating platforms to manage bidding workflows and subcontractor outreach

 - Assist project management teams across the project lifecycle as needed



Required Qualifications

 - 2–3+ years of experience in construction estimating (flexible based on overall background)

 - Experience in general contracting or multi-trade commercial bidding

 - Strong understanding of construction pricing, cost breakdowns, and subcontractor coordination

 - Experience with national chain construction standards and repeat-project pricing models

 - Familiarity with regional market pricing, especially Florida, Arizona, Las Vegas, and Colorado

 - Experience supporting or collaborating with project management functions

 - Proficiency in PlanHub and/or PlanSwift estimating software

 - Excellent English communication skills (written and verbal)

 - Ability to work independently in a remote, fast-paced environment



Preferred Attributes

 - Strong analytical and attention-to-detail skills

 - Ability to manage multiple bids simultaneously under deadlines

 - Experience working with U.S.-based general contractors

 - Strong organizational and time management skills

 - Proactive communication and ability to coordinate across distributed teams



Work Environment

 - Fully remote role (LATAM-based)

 - Direct collaboration with U.S.-based estimating and project management teams

 - Fast-paced construction bidding environment with recurring national projects

Compensation: USD $1,500 - $2,500/month (depending on seniority)



About the Company

Our client is a fast-growing, multi-faceted construction and custom building company based in Reno, Nevada. In addition to high-end custom residential builds and commercial contracting, they operate a specialized paint division and an in-house proprietary cabinet line. Operating with a lean, efficient team structure, the company is built on absolute transparency and honesty—firmly rejecting the industry trend of under-bidding projects just to hit clients with change orders later. They focus on delivering meticulously accurate, value-driven bids that build long-term trust with their clients.



Job Description

We are seeking a highly meticulous and analytical Remote Construction Estimator to join a growing operations team. In this role, you will work directly alongside the business owner to take over the day-to-day estimating pipeline, freeing up leadership to focus on field management and scaling operations. You will own the full takeoff and pre-construction lifecycle for both high-end custom homes ($1M–$3.2M+) and commercial tenant improvements (TI). The ideal candidate is a "nitty-gritty" detail guru who practices intense due diligence, ensuring no finish, specialized trade, or scope detail is ever missed.



Responsibilities

 - Perform comprehensive and highly accurate material takeoffs from structural and architectural plans for high-end custom residential builds and commercial build-outs.

 - Manage the complete pre-construction pipeline, including thorough bid preparation, compiling submittal packages, managing RFIs, and tracking document revisions.

 - Lead vendor outreach and subcontractor coordination to source reliable, honest pricing and eliminate downstream scope gaps.

 - Integrate data smoothly into the client's internal processes and learn to navigate their custom-built, proprietary bidding software/system.

 - Meticulously review project documentation to execute bulletproof double-checks, ensuring final bids are fully transparent and inclusive of all specialized components.



Project Types

 - High-end custom residential builds ($1M–$3.2M+ scope)

 - Commercial tenant improvements (TI) and full commercial build-outs

 - Commercial kitchen builds (incorporating complex systems like commercial exhaust hoods and specialized equipment)

 - Multi-divisional coordination involving custom cabinetry and specialized finishes



Requirements

 - Experience: 3 to 10+ years of dedicated construction estimating experience, with extensive exposure to U.S.-based projects and market context.

 - Educational Background: Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering, Construction Management, or a closely related technical field.

 - Niche Expertise: Demonstrated experience estimating high-end custom residential projects and complex commercial build-outs (commercial kitchen estimation experience is highly valued).

 - Software Proficiency: Highly fluent in industry-standard takeoff tools such as Bluebeam, PlanSwift, or On-Screen Takeoff (OST), with a strong aptitude for adapting to proprietary digital systems.

 - Communication & Alignment: Absolute bilingual fluency in English (written and spoken) for seamless internal reporting and professional vendor/subcontractor collaboration.

source_jobs.title: Tax Staff Accountant

TAX ASSOCIATE / STAFF ACCOUNTANT

Job Title: Experienced Tax Staff

Location: Remote (Philippines, Central America/Latin America)

We are a U.S.-based commercial general contracting company specializing in retail, restaurant, office, medical, hospitality, and tenant improvement projects nationwide. Our team works closely across preconstruction, estimating, and project management to deliver high-quality construction solutions from bid to build.



We are built on a collaborative, fast-paced environment where accuracy, coordination, and accountability are key. Our focus is on delivering consistent results for repeat clients and national brands through strong subcontractor partnerships, detailed estimating, and efficient project execution.



Position Overview

We are seeking a detail-oriented and experienced Construction Estimator to join our general contracting team in a full-time remote capacity. In this role, you will be embedded within our organization, working closely with project management and pre-construction teams to support national bidding and estimation efforts across the United States.



You will play a key role in preparing accurate cost estimates, coordinating subcontractor pricing, and supporting end-to-end bid preparation for commercial construction projects.



Key Responsibilities

 - Perform detailed quantity takeoffs and material analysis from construction drawings and specifications

 - Prepare accurate and competitive cost estimates for commercial bid submissions

 - Review, analyze, and prequalify subcontractor bids to ensure full trade coverage and pricing competitiveness

 - Coordinate with subcontractors and sub-trade partners to obtain complete and up-to-date pricing

 - Support the full bid preparation and submission process

 - Collaborate with project managers during contract review and post-award project setup

 - Manage RFIs (Requests for Information) and submittals during the preconstruction phase

 - Use PlanHub and other estimating platforms to manage bidding workflows and subcontractor outreach

 - Assist project management teams across the project lifecycle as needed



Required Qualifications

 - 2–3+ years of experience in construction estimating (flexible based on overall background)

 - Experience in general contracting or multi-trade commercial bidding

 - Strong understanding of construction pricing, cost breakdowns, and subcontractor coordination

 - Experience with national chain construction standards and repeat-project pricing models

 - Familiarity with regional market pricing, especially Florida, Arizona, Las Vegas, and Colorado

 - Experience supporting or collaborating with project management functions

 - Proficiency in PlanHub and/or PlanSwift estimating software

 - Excellent English communication skills (written and verbal)

 - Ability to work independently in a remote, fast-paced environment



Preferred Attributes

 - Strong analytical and attention-to-detail skills

 - Ability to manage multiple bids simultaneously under deadlines

 - Experience working with U.S.-based general contractors

 - Strong organizational and time management skills

 - Proactive communication and ability to coordinate across distributed teams



Work Environment

 - Fully remote role (LATAM-based)

 - Direct collaboration with U.S.-based estimating and project management teams

 - Fast-paced construction bidding environment with recurring national projects

Location: LATAM



About the Company

Our client is an established commercial landscape construction contractor specializing in site work, hardscape construction, concrete, fencing, and large-scale outdoor projects across the United States. Operating as both a general contractor and subcontractor, they are known for delivering high-quality work through a detail-oriented and collaborative approach to pre-construction and project execution.



About the Role

We are looking for a Commercial Landscape Estimator with strong experience estimating U.S. commercial construction projects involving site work, fencing, concrete, and landscape construction scopes.



This is not a takeoff-only position. The ideal candidate will bring critical thinking, pricing strategy, subcontractor coordination experience, and the ability to identify scope gaps and project risks before submission.

Responsibilities

MARKETING ASSISTANT

 - Location: LATAM (Remote)

 - Compensation: USD $2,500/month


ABOUT THE COMPANY:

For over 35 years, we've transacted more than $3.5 billion of real estate, acquiring over 14,648 multifamily units and taking dozens of properties full cycle — on average returning 100% of investor capital plus strong returns. Our focus is acquiring older apartment home communities and creating value by improving the properties, optimizing operations, and fostering human flourishing in each one. Above all, we've learned that success is measured not just by financial gain, but by the quality of the relationships we build along the way. Please read our values https://wearewilkinson.com/about/real-estate-investment-group-values, and if you are aligned, we encourage you to apply.

 


JOB OVERVIEW

We're a US-based investment firm running two brands — an SEC-regulated real-estate investment fund and a family-focused legacy coaching and publishing brand. The Marketing Operations Administrator is the logistics and back-office engine: marketing administrative systems are managed, events coordinated end-to-end, spreadsheets and systems put in place, and inventory, print, shipping, and scheduling functions never slip. This seat exists to keep coordinator-level work off the senior seats and to free up the EVP's time — when it's done well, leadership stops thinking about logistics entirely. Events are hands-on and on-site, so reliable travel is occasional.





KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Support the EVP of Marketing directly. Act as the EVP's assistant and right hand: triage and follow up on action items, prepare briefing materials before meetings and meetups, track commitments to closure, and take on ad hoc work to keep marketing priorities on track.

 - Own the money trail. Confirm event budgets and reconcile against actual spend; manage invoice collection and the 25th-of-month payment cycle; records management for cancellations, deposits, and revised quotes; gather and GL-code vendor invoices before each payment run; input cardholder receipts (Ramp) and code them monthly; maintain the spend spreadsheet against the cap; audit the SaaS tool list and flag charges that no longer pull weight.

 - Manage inventory, print, and fulfillment. Manage in house assistants to tally marketing inventory (restock, storage, shelving); source and order branded promo items and track delivery; manage print jobs and pricing; ship workbooks, books, and equipment with tracking and stated deadlines; coordinate workbook fulfillment and reprints; administer orders end-to-end.

 - Keep the back office running. Track vendor contracts and IC agreements (Docusign admin); coordinate schedules across the principals, the Traffic Manager, and the team; request and track tool access.

 - Run events and conferences end-to-end. Own events from overseeing an event coordinator or if smaller, complete the work yourself for: venue, catering, linens, and signage to AV; book conference travel, booth, and material shipping; make sure business cards and NFC cards are printed and on hand; set up and verify AV and the on-site presentation; set up and break down the booth.


REQUIREMENTS

 - 5+ years in marketing operations, field marketing, or a logistics-and-admin role with real ownership of projects, events and budgets.

 - Excellent understanding of Google workspace, setting up spreadsheets, saas billing, how to edit DNS, email protocols, English spelling and grammar, and most importantly AI (not just chat, but agentic and connectors).

 - Event and conference production: venue, catering, AV, booth, travel and shipping logistics, and confident on-site execution.

source_jobs.title: Glass Estimator
source_jobs.description:
Type: Full-Time, Remote (LATAM)

Working Hours: Mondays - Fridays, 9:00am - 5:00pm CST

Location: LATAM (Remote)

Compensation: USD $1,500 - $2,500/month (depending on seniority)



About the Company

Our client is a highly respected, family-owned glass and glazing company based in New Orleans, Louisiana, with deep industry roots dating back to 1956. Specializing in large-scale institutional, healthcare, and commercial projects, they are a regional leader in hurricane impact-rated glass systems. Operating with a lean, close-knit team, the company values mentorship, knowledge-sharing, and collaboration, and is looking for a dedicated estimator to support their next chapter of business growth.



Job Description

We are seeking an analytical and detail-oriented Remote Estimator to join a seasoned pre-construction team. In this role, you will work closely with senior leadership to augment their bidding capacity, independently owning the estimating process for mid-sized projects ($150K–$500K) while providing vital support on large-scale institutional bids exceeding $2M–$5M+. This is an incredible opportunity for a professional looking to leverage their technical expertise within a stable, supportive U.S. construction framework that values continuous learning and long-term career growth.



Responsibilities

 - Prepare highly detailed quantity takeoffs and accurate cost estimates specifically for commercial glass, glazing, and storefront systems.

 - Manage end-to-end bid documentation workflows, ensuring all bid preparation requirements and local guidelines are meticulously met.

 - Calculate accurate labor cost projections, project timelines, and logistical variables, including specialized assessments for high-rise work requiring swing stages and lifts.

 - Lead vendor outreach and request for quotes (RFQs), building and maintaining strong coordination with major U.S. suppliers like YKK, US Aluminum/CR Lawrence, and Old Castle Building Envelope.

 - Support pre-construction logistics, compile submittals, and manage RFIs to ensure a seamless handoff to operations teams.

 - Utilize Bloom & Beam OST software (or similar estimating platforms) to maintain organized, data-driven pipelines.



Project Types

 - Large institutional and healthcare facilities

 - Commercial office buildings and high-rise developments

 - Hurricane impact-rated glass and resilient building systems

 - Complex mid-to-large-scale storefront and curtain wall installations ($150K to $5M+ scope)



Requirements

 - Experience & Background: 2+ years of dedicated glass and glazing estimating experience on U.S. construction projects, OR a strong background in Civil Engineering with transferable structural estimating skills.

 - Software Proficiency: Hands-on experience with estimating software—Bloom & Beam OST is highly preferred, but equivalent takeoff platforms are accepted.

 - Technical Knowledge: Strong ability to read and interpret complex architectural and structural plans, evaluate labor dynamics, and handle multi-million-dollar bids.

 - Communication & Alignment: Exceptional, fluent English communication skills (written and verbal) for accurate internal collaboration and vendor outreach.

 - Schedule: Full-time availability strictly matching U.S. Central Time (CT) operations.

 - Market Exposure: Prior experience with hurricane-resilient building systems or projects in the Gulf Coast, Caribbean, or Puerto Rico markets is a massive plus.

source_jobs.description:
Job Title: Mechanical Estimator / Pre-Construction Support Specialist

Employment Type: Full-Time, Remote

Working Hours: Monday to Friday, 8 AM - 5 PM US Eastern Time

Compensation: USD $1,500 – $2,200 per month (depending on experience)

Location: LATAM



About the Company

Our client is a full-service mechanical contractor based in Georgia, serving commercial clients throughout the Southeast United States. They specialize in HVAC, mechanical piping, ductwork, design-build, renovations, new construction, and ongoing service projects. With a collaborative, hands-on team, they are committed to delivering high-quality mechanical solutions while maintaining strong relationships with clients, vendors, and subcontractors. As the company continues to grow, they are looking for a skilled Mechanical Estimator to support their pre-construction operations and help deliver accurate, competitive project estimates.



About the Role

We are seeking a detail-oriented Mechanical Estimator / Pre-Construction Support Specialist with experience in commercial mechanical construction estimating. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys both technical estimating and pre-construction coordination, with a strong understanding of HVAC ductwork and mechanical piping systems.



You'll work closely with Project Managers to prepare competitive bids, perform accurate quantity takeoffs, coordinate with vendors and subcontractors, and support projects throughout the pre-construction phase.



Key Responsibilities

 - Perform accurate quantity takeoffs for commercial mechanical systems, including ductwork and piping for new installations.

 - Prepare detailed material estimates using company pricing templates and Excel-based estimating tools.

 - Assist with bid preparation, proposal documentation, and pre-construction planning.

 - Manage RFIs, submittals, specification reviews, and code revisions throughout the bidding process.

 - Coordinate with vendors and subcontractors to obtain pricing, product information, and quotations.

 - Review project plans, specifications, and construction documents to identify scope and potential risks.

 - Maintain organized estimating records and documentation.

 - Collaborate with Project Managers while working independently in a fast-paced remote environment.

 - Utilize construction management platforms such as Procore and digital takeoff software throughout the estimating process.



Qualifications

 - Bachelor's degree in Civil Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Construction Management, or a closely related technical field (preferred).

 - 2–3 years of professional experience estimating commercial mechanical construction projects in the US market.

 - Strong understanding of HVAC systems, ductwork, piping, and mechanical construction methods.

 - Experience performing digital quantity takeoffs from construction drawings.

 - Proficiency with Microsoft Excel, including building and maintaining estimating templates.

 - Experience using Procore or similar construction management software.

 - Excellent analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills.

 - Strong written and spoken English communication skills.

 - Comfortable working full-time during US Eastern Time business hours.



Preferred Qualifications

 - Experience independently creating or improving estimating spreadsheets and pricing templates.

 - Strong knowledge of commercial mechanical systems and construction workflows.

 - Experience coordinating directly with vendors and subcontractors during the bidding process.

 - Ability to manage multiple estimates simultaneously while meeting deadlines.
